channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: EU top diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah in US-French deal
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korean man convicted for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean man has been sentenced to a suspended prison term for deliberately gaining more than 20 kilograms (44 pounds) to evade a tougher role in the country’s military conscription system, a Seoul court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 Uzbek nationals arrested in the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi in the UAE
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates said Monday police arrested three Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi, an attack that's raised concerns for the burgeoning Israeli community in the country.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' resentencing plea
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez will have to wait until next year for a decision on whether they should have the possibility of freedom from prison more than 30 years after killing their parents, a judge said. The shotgun killings of Jose and Kitty Menendez on Aug.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 24-year-old man dies while hanging Christmas lights at San Diego suburb home
item.description: A 24-year-old man died by electrocution while installing Christmas lights on the roof of a home in Southern California, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Diddy uses Donald Trump's criminal case in bail argument
item.description: Combs’ lawyers said his pretrial speech should be evaluated based on the standard used in Trump's criminal case.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: The Onion's bid for Alex Jones' Infowars hangs in the balance as judge orders new hearing
item.description: A bankruptcy judge on Monday delayed a hearing in conspiracy theorist Alex Jones’ effort to stop the satirical news outlet The Onion from buying Infowars, keeping the auction sale up in the air for at least another few weeks.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge in LA delays until January decision on resentencing Menendez brothers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge on Monday delayed until January his decision on whether to resentence Erik and Lyle Menendez for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion 35 years ago, squashing their family’s hope the brothers would be released and home for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White Florida woman sentenced to 25 years in prison for shooting Black neighbor in lengthy dispute
item.description: A white Florida woman who fatally shot a Black neighbor through her front door during an ongoing dispute over the neighbor’s boisterous children was sentenced Monday to 25 years in prison for her manslaughter conviction. Susan Lorincz, 60, was convicted in August of killing Ajike “A.J.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At the White House, it’s beginning to look a lot like Christmas
item.description: The first lady invited military-connected families to join her on her final turn of decking the White House halls.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: MAP: Every state's favorite Thanksgiving side, according to Campbell's
item.description: The Thanksgiving turkey won’t be the star of the show in some households this holiday season, according to a recent study.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference case against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge on Monday to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election, citing longstanding Justice Department policy shielding presidents from prosecution while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAQs surrounding credit card debt
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time to provide the most pertinent questions and answers.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Jack Smith files to drop Jan. 6 charges against Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has filed a motion to drop all four felony charges against President-elect Donald Trump in connection with his effort to overturn his 2020 presidential election in the lead-up to the deadly Jan. 6 attack on the U.S Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly and Ken Dilanian, NBC News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bird flu detected in raw milk from California, state officials warn
item.description: The avian influenza virus has been detected in a retail sample of raw milk from a California-based raw dairy farm, California health officials confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Varela, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First right whales of season gorge on critical food off Massachusetts, giving hope for a strong year
item.description: Scientists who study a critically endangered species of whale that lives off New England said encouraging early signs suggest the animals could have a strong season for feeding and breeding.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Father of Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles found dead near LAX
item.description: Ryan Kobayashi, father of a 31-year-old Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles nearly two weeks ago, was found dead near Los Angeles International Airport early Sunday morning, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Time running out for Senate to pass Social Security Fairness Act
item.description: The House recently passed a Social Security bill but the Senate is running out of time to get it over the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Thanksgiving Travel Latest: Airport strike, staff shortages and weather could impact holiday travel
item.description: Airports and highways are expected to be jam-packed during Thanksgiving week, a holiday period likely to end with another record day for air travel in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden pardons turkeys Peach and Blossom for his last Thanksgiving event
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday pardoned the turkeys Peach and Blossom for the traditional presidential pardon ahead of Thanksgiving, which marks the start of Biden’s last holiday season at the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Macy's says employee hid up to $154 million in expenses
item.description: The company added that the person behind the conduct is no longer an employee and that the investigation didn't identify involvement by any other worker.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: GOP senator: Americans don’t care about FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.), who was rumored to be on Trump’s shortlist to serve as Treasury secretary, said most Americans “don’t care” whether Trump uses the FBI to vet his nominees, something fellow Republican senators have demanded.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Workers at Charlotte airport, an American Airlines hub, go on strike during Thanksgiving travel week
item.description: American Airlines says it doesn’t expect significant disruptions to flights this week as a result of a labor strike at its hub in Charlotte, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Erik Verduzco, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans push back against Democrats' claims that Trump intelligence pick Gabbard is compromised
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Republican senators pushed back on Sunday against criticism from Democrats that Tulsi Gabbard, Donald Trump's pick to lead U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What's behind the doors of this Missouri cave? A former roller rink, concert stage and more
item.description: Adventure awaits at every turn of one captivating cave in suburban St. Louis, now modernized into a quirky and nostalgic getaway with a storied legacy.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Bishop T.D. Jakes experiences 'health incident' during Sunday service
item.description: Bishop T.D. Jakes experienced a "slight health incident" while wrapping up his sermon at the Potter's House of Dallas on Sunday, the church confirmed in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Earth bids farewell to its temporary 'mini moon': What it was, and where it's going
item.description: Currently, more than 2 million miles away, the object is too small and faint to see without a powerful telescope.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Salad topping sold in Florida, 21 other states recalled over wrong ingredient
item.description: The packagers should contain tortilla strips but instead, consumers have found crispy onion strips inside.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: You can get paid $2,500 to shop at Costco for a week
item.description: Attention Costco shoppers! How would you like to get paid $2,500 plus a free Costco membership to shop at your favorite store?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: Chuck Woolery, former host of 'Wheel of Fortune' and more, dies
item.description: Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
